reviewScope: same-sample V40.3R2 stylized IP-Adapter candidates compared against V39 target experience requirements

observations: The stylized retry reduces some indoor room clutter, but the candidate still reads as semi-photographic generated cat images rather than a clean desktop-pet sprite action pack.
Multiple frames contain bright accessory-like artifacts around the mouth, chest, or body.
The eight action poses remain weak: eat, play, alert, and celebrate are not clearly readable as distinct action states.
The output is not reliably normalizable into a product action pack and is not clearly better than the V39 target-experience baseline.
The stylized retry creates a more illustrated tuxedo cat, but action semantics are still not reliable across all eight actions.
Several frames include container, panel, or mark-like artifacts that would pollute a desktop-pet sprite pack.
Eat, play, alert, and celebrate do not read as robust action states at desktop-pet scale.
The candidate is visually more pleasant than the first retry but still fails the V40 requirement for accepted same-sample action assets.

claimBoundary: This review does not claim high-quality image-to-action asset generation, arbitrary-cat readiness, Petdex parity, provider integration, WebUI readiness, ComfyUI readiness, 3D readiness, production readiness, Windows readiness, or cross-platform readiness.
securityBoundary: The review records safe relative candidate identifiers and qualitative observations only.
